title: 1.6.4: very noisy output; many "proto: duplicate proto type registered: " warnings
body:
Podman 1.6.3 and 1.6.4 became very noisy on every operation printing many `proto: duplicate proto type registered:` lines to `STDERR` before meaningful output of _any_ command:

<details>
<summary>expand</summary>

~~~~
$ podman images                                                                                 
2020/01/04 09:50:53 proto: duplicate proto type registered: k8s.io.apimachinery.pkg.api.resource.Quantity                                                    
2020/01/04 09:50:53 proto: duplicate proto type registered: k8s.io.apimachinery.pkg.runtime.RawExtension

comments.body: What OS? Did you build Podman yourself, or did it come from a package?
Built from source on Debian "unstable" for the upcoming official package. Had no such problem on 1.6.2 so it appears to be a regression somewhere...

Have not seen this anywhere else, so I suspect it may be a packaging issue. It seems like this is out of either github.com/gogo/protobuf or github.com/golang/protobuf, both of which are indirect dependencies (but appear an alarming number of times, with an alarming number of versions, in go.mod).

Definitely not a packaging issue and strangely enough relevant protobuf packages did not change recently...
The only thing different is newer libpod + buildah and `containers/image`.

Also "coreos/bbolt" was updated to 1.3.3 but I doubt that could cause such effect...

I have 1.6.4 from https://software.opensuse.org//download.html?project=devel%3Akubic%3Alibcontainers%3Astable&package=podman.  Forgive me if it is not relevant but on Debian "stable" I see:

```
$ podman images

REPOSITORY                         TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
quay.io/jdeathe/centos-ssh-redis   latest   be2a8a7f51c5   2 months ago    251 MB
docker.io/library/centos           latest   0f3e07c0138f   3 months ago    227 MB
docker.io/rastasheep/ubuntu-sshd   latest   49533628fb37   19 months ago   242 MB
```

@onlyjob do you have a version diff of dependencies in the Debian build compared to what we vendor? I suspect that the Debian build includes a package in a different version that introduces it. Knowing which dependencies differ version-wise could help reveal what's wrong. Otherwise, I could only guess.

I can not spot what would be relevant here...

> + golang-gogoprotobuf-dev (= 1.2.1+git20190611.dadb6258-1),

^ this looks suspicious.

Some bells are ringing ... we have seen that at some point last year.

Tracked the problem to `github.com/gogo/protobuf` by building v1.6.4 against (un-)vendored library:

v1.6.2: v1.2.2-0.20190723190241-65acae22fc9d
v.1.6.4: v1.2.2-0.20190723190241-65acae22fc9d
system: 1.2.1+git20190611.dadb6258-1

Interestingly version of `golang-github-gogo-protobuf-dev` did not change so it must be the use of the library is somehow different. But `ack github.com/gogo/protobuf` only finds it under `vendor/k8s.io` and the only difference there between 1.6.2 and 1.6.4 is the following:

~~~~
--- a/libpod-1.6.4/vendor/k8s.io/client-go/pkg/version/base.go
+++ b/libpod-1.6.4/vendor/k8s.io/client-go/pkg/version/base.go
@@ -58,2 +58,2 @@
-       gitVersion   string = "v0.0.0-master+f3ffda1e08"
-       gitCommit    string = "f3ffda1e08f19e9a6a88484136b5eed76533f21a" // sha1 from git, output of $(git rev-parse HEAD)
+       gitVersion   string = "v0.0.0-master+5cc92849f7"
+       gitCommit    string = "5cc92849f7fc9dd734ca2fd8f3ae8830b9a7eb26" // sha1 from git, output of $(git rev-parse HEAD)
~~~~

I guess something fishy must be happening in indirect dependencies that consume `github.com/gogo/protobuf`...

Ah, I've found a workaround and it is so weird that it is nearly insane...

First I compared system `github.com/gogo/protobuf` to vendored one and here is

<details>
<summary>the only irrelevant difference</summary>

~~~~
$ diff -U0 /usr/share/gocode/src/github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto/extensions_gogo.go vendor/github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto/extensions_gogo.go
--- /usr/share/gocode/src/github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto/extensions_gogo.go     2019-07-02 04:35:16.000000000 +1000
+++ vendor/github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto/extensions_gogo.go    2019-12-12 04:22:17.000000000 +1100
@@ -156,0 +157,4 @@
+func EncodeInternalExtensionBackwards(m extendableProto, data []byte) (n int, err error) {
+       return EncodeExtensionMapBackwards(m.extensionsWrite(), data)
+}
+
@@ -166,0 +171,17 @@
+               o += n
+       }
+       return o, nil
+}
+
+func EncodeExtensionMapBackwards(m map[int32]Extension, data []byte) (n int, err error) {
+       o := 0
+       end := len(data)
+       for _, e := range m {
+               if err := e.Encode(); err != nil {
+                       return 0, err
+               }
+               n := copy(data[end-len(e.enc):], e.enc)
+               if n != len(e.enc) {
+                       return 0, io.ErrShortBuffer
+               }
+               end -= n
~~~~
</details>

Then I replaced vendored `github.com/gogo/protobuf` with the system one in `vendor` directory and it "fixed" the problem.

The way we build Golang packages on Debian is by removing libraries from `vendor` in favour of re-usable system libraries available from GOPATH. In most cases there is only one version of any given library in GOPATH but sometimes `vendor` directory have different version of a private library that takes precedence over system one. 

In regards to this particular problem, to make it go away I had to symlink system `github.com/gogo/protobuf` into vendor directory. That's it, merely having the same code (either version) in `vendor` directory -- the very same library that is already available from GOPATH, yields a different run-time behaviour depending on where the library is included from (`vendor` or GOPATH).

It makes no sense to me why/how on Earth the run-time behaviour can differ depending on whether a library (the same code) is included from GOPATH or from `vendor`. But that's the way it is, at least on Golang 1.13.3 and 1.13.5.

I hope that Golang gurus can explain this anomaly??
Could it be that both versions of protobuf were used before symlinking (i.e., GOPATH and vendor)?
No. Only one. Simply moving it to `vendor` made the difference.
That was the only explanation I could come up with. Very curious! I am glad you could fix it but let's keep an eye open to hopefully shed some light on this issue.

I'm not sure... On one hand at this time I recognise no action that maintainers can take. But on another hand due to probable Golang bug Libpod requires a very specific and non-obvious build workaround. This is definitely an problem that ideally should be addressed this way or another.
I would keep this issue open until it is better understood.
Well if this happens again we can reopen.
It will certainly happen again as soon as I remove highly unusual workaround: https://salsa.debian.org/debian/libpod/blob/master/debian/rules#L34-39
